notes: {not, and} is complete because {not, and, or} is (thm:standard-connectives), or is definable from {not, and} by De Morgan (thm:de-morgan-laws), and replacement (thm:replacement-of-logical-equivalents) substitutes it in. All three load-bearing; def:functionally-complete is the target notion, transitive via thm:standard-connectives.
